text:
You place your hand on my chest, but your gaze is turned elsewhere;
If you must look, then look with heart—is that too much to bear?

My final breath is leaving now, a sight for you to hold;
You bring a mirror to my lips, but your own face you behold.

Don't ask about this wasting state, this weakness plain to see;
Are you observing my decline, or your own symmetry?

The candle-tree is ringed with wings of moths that flew too near;
This is the fruit of love's long fall, the harvest that you see here.

When you observe the willow, named for Majnun's endless grief,
Do you, O seers, see the man beyond the trembling leaf?

The Ghost of Longing came to stand beside my corpse to jeer:
"Whose path do you still watch for now? Why do you gaze from here?"
